mnemonics.title: PEAS Components
mnemonics.content: P-E-A-S: Please Explain Agent's Sensors - Performance, Environment, Actuators, Sensors are the four components needed to specify any task environment.

mnemonics.title: Environment Properties
mnemonics.content: D-O-P-S-I: Deterministic/ Stochastic, Observable/Partially Observable, Performance measure, Single/Multi-agent, Episodic/Sequential, Static/Dynamic, Discrete/Continuous - covers all 8 environment properties.

mnemonics.title: Agent Types Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: From Simple to Smart: Reflex → Model-Based → Goal-Based → Utility-Based - each adds more intelligence (state, goals, utility).

acronyms.usage: Framework to specify task environments for AI agents
Used when environment is partially observable and stochastic
Field of study of rational agents

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box representing the agent, with sensors pointing from environment to agent (percepts), and actuators pointing from agent to environment (actions). Arrows show flow direction.
Use a pyramid: Base = Simple Reflex (smallest), Middle = Model-Based, Goal-Based, Top = Utility-Based (most complex/capable).
Create a 2x2 grid: X-axis = Observable to Partially Observable, Y-axis = Deterministic to Stochastic, placing examples like Chess, Poker, Taxi at appropriate positions.

analogies.analogy: Just as humans use eyes (sensors) to see and hands (actuators) to interact, AI agents use sensors and actuators to interact with their environment.
The agent function is like the brain - it takes input (percepts) and decides output (actions). The agent program is the specific implementation of this brain.
The environment is like the rules of a game - they define what actions are possible and what outcomes result from those actions.

analogies.concept: Rational Agent as Smart Decision Maker
analogies.analogy: A rational agent is like a chess player who thinks ahead, considering which move leads to the best chance of winning (maximizing performance).
